================================================================================
## Game jam: City Builder Weekend Jam 2024
URL: https://www.vibecode.game/game-jams/city-builder-weekend-jam-2024
Source: src/content/game-jams/en/city-builder-weekend-jam-2024.mdx
================================================================================

Description: A weekend jam dedicated to compact city-building concepts with strong readability and strategic depth.

## Theme

**Grow Under Pressure**. Build a city-building experience where every expansion introduces a meaningful trade-off.

## Event Parameters

- Duration: 48 hours
- Team size: 1-3 people
- Platforms: WebGL preferred, desktop accepted
- Mandatory feature: one district-level decision with visible long-term impact

## Prize Breakdown

Total support package value: **EUR 8,000**

- Best Strategic Depth - EUR 3,000
- Best UI Readability - EUR 2,000
- Best Audio Atmosphere - EUR 1,500
- Community Choice - EUR 1,500

## Submission Workflow

1. Upload playable build and source summary.
2. Provide one map seed used for evaluation.
3. Include a balancing note describing your economy loop.
4. Submit by 20:00 CEST on June 16.

## Judging Dimensions

- Strategy clarity
- Information hierarchy and UX
- Replayability and balancing
- Production completeness

## Submission Highlights

- **Block by Block** - a district zoning sim where transit debt impacts citizen happiness.
- **Harborline** - a coastal logistics builder with weather-driven resource volatility.
- **Night Shift Mayor** - a pressure-management city sim with strict 5-minute rounds.

## Final Results

- **Winner:** Harborline (Team Delta Harbor)
- **2nd Place:** Block by Block (Team Gridform)
- **Community Choice:** Night Shift Mayor (Team Owl Clock)

The jury emphasized Harborline's clean UI and excellent risk-reward balancing across short sessions.

================================================================================
## Game jam: EU Cozy Automation Jam 2026
URL: https://www.vibecode.game/game-jams/eu-cozy-automation-jam-2026
Source: src/content/game-jams/en/eu-cozy-automation-jam-2026.mdx
================================================================================

Description: A hybrid game jam in Berlin for cozy systems design, low-pressure progression, and satisfying automation loops.

## Theme

**Small Worlds, Smart Systems**. Participants create short-form games where automation helps the player nurture, craft, or restore a space.

## Format

This jam runs as a hybrid event:

- In-person hub at MotionLab Berlin
- Remote participation via Discord and daily office hours

## Rules

- Team size: 1-4 people
- Engine/tooling: open choice
- Accessibility requirement: include at least one accessibility option (text size, key rebinding, color mode, etc.)
- Content requirement: no explicit violence or hateful content

## Timeline

- **Theme reveal:** Sep 18, 17:00 CEST
- **Mentor clinic:** Sep 21 and Sep 24
- **Final upload cutoff:** Sep 27, 21:00 CEST
- **Demo night in Berlin:** Oct 2

## Prize Categories

- Best Cozy Loop - EUR 2,500
- Best Automation Design - EUR 2,000
- Best Accessibility - EUR 1,500
- Community Favorite - EUR 1,000

Additional sponsor rewards include playtest sessions with partner studios and Steam page review feedback.

## Submission Checklist

1. Upload build and screenshots to your Itch.io page.
2. Add design notes documenting your automation system.
3. Include a one-page controls reference.
4. Mark the entry as solo or team, and list contributors.

## Judging Rubric

- Clarity of systems
- Emotional tone consistency
- Accessibility and UX
- Technical execution

================================================================================
## Game jam: Global Arcade Sprint 2027
URL: https://www.vibecode.game/game-jams/global-arcade-sprint-2027
Source: src/content/game-jams/en/global-arcade-sprint-2027.mdx
================================================================================

Description: A 10-day international online game jam focused on score-driven arcade loops and instant replayability.

## Theme

**One More Run**. Build an arcade experience where the core loop is clear in under 20 seconds and players immediately want to retry.

## Eligibility and Team Rules

- Solo developers and teams up to 5 members are welcome.
- Entries must be built during the jam window.
- Reused assets are allowed if disclosed on your submission page.
- AI-assisted workflows are allowed, but the game design and final integration decisions must be your own.

## Schedule

- **Kickoff stream:** Feb 5, 18:00 UTC
- **Mid-jam playtest checkpoint:** Feb 10, 19:00 UTC
- **Submission deadline:** Feb 14, 23:59 UTC
- **Community voting:** Feb 15-20
- **Jury finals showcase:** Feb 23

## Prize Pool

Total prize pool: **$12,500**

- 1st Place: $6,000 + publishing mentorship package
- 2nd Place: $3,000
- 3rd Place: $1,500
- Best Audio Feel: $1,000
- Best Onboarding: $1,000

Sponsored by Studio Vertex, ByteRift Audio, and InputLab.

## Submission Process

1. Publish your game on Itch.io with downloadable build and web build (if available).
2. Include a 60-90 second gameplay video.
3. Add a short postmortem covering core mechanic, tools used, and technical risks solved.
4. Tag your page with `global-arcade-sprint-2027`.

## Judging Criteria

- **Gameplay Loop (40%)**: Is the loop immediately understandable and replayable?
- **Game Feel (25%)**: Input responsiveness, juice, and audiovisual feedback.
- **Innovation (20%)**: Fresh mechanic or twist within arcade constraints.
- **Polish (15%)**: Stability, clarity, and onboarding quality.

================================================================================
## Game jam: SEA Mobile Microjam 2026
URL: https://www.vibecode.game/game-jams/seasia-mobile-microjam-2026
Source: src/content/game-jams/en/seasia-mobile-microjam-2026.mdx
================================================================================

Description: A fast, mobile-first jam focused on one-handed control schemes and session lengths under 3 minutes.

## Theme

**Thumb-Only Tension**. Create a game that can be played with one thumb while preserving meaningful decision-making.

## Current Phase

The jam is **currently in progress**. Daily standups, feedback channels, and mentor sessions are active in Discord.

## Constraints

- Portrait mode preferred (landscape allowed with strong justification)
- Target session duration: 60-180 seconds
- At least one progression layer (unlock, score chase, or run modifiers)
- All submissions must run on Android WebView or mobile browser

## Ongoing Schedule

- **Kickoff:** Apr 20, 19:00 SGT
- **Playtest sprint:** Apr 25, 20:00 SGT
- **Submission deadline:** Apr 30, 23:59 SGT

## Prize Track

Total rewards: **SGD 9,000**

- Best Mobile Feel - SGD 3,500
- Best Short Session Loop - SGD 2,500
- Best Monetization-Free Design - SGD 1,500
- Rising Team Award - SGD 1,500

## Submission Process

1. Submit a web-playable link and APK backup.
2. Include a 30-second portrait gameplay clip.
3. Publish your control schema and accessibility settings.
4. Tag entry with `sea-mobile-microjam-2026`.

## Judging Criteria

- Input clarity and responsiveness
- Replayability in short sessions
- Stability on mid-range devices
- Originality within mobile constraints

================================================================================
## Game jam: Winter Prototype Showdown 2025
URL: https://www.vibecode.game/game-jams/winter-prototype-showdown-2025
Source: src/content/game-jams/en/winter-prototype-showdown-2025.mdx
================================================================================

Description: A 7-day rapid prototyping jam for experimental mechanics, culminating in a live finalist showcase.

## Theme

**Broken Rules, Better Games**. Teams were encouraged to intentionally violate one common design convention and build around the consequences.

## Participation Snapshot

- 428 registered participants
- 136 submitted prototypes
- 22 countries represented

## Prize Pool

Total: **CAD 15,000**

- Grand Jury Winner: CAD 7,000
- Runner-up: CAD 4,000
- Experimental Design Award: CAD 2,000
- Best Student Team: CAD 2,000

## Submission Process Used

Participants submitted:

1. playable build (web or desktop),
2. a 2-minute narrated walkthrough,
3. a one-page design intent note,
4. a known-issues list for judges.

## Judging Criteria

- Design risk and originality (35%)
- Playability and onboarding (30%)
- Technical execution (20%)
- Presentation quality (15%)

## Submission Highlights

- **Signal//Lost** - asynchronous co-op puzzle where each player sees different rule sets.
- **Parallax Drift** - racing prototype with delayed input prediction as core mechanic.
- **Borrowed HP** - action deckbuilder where health can be traded for rule changes.

## Final Results

- **Winner:** Signal//Lost (Team Frostbyte)
- **Runner-up:** Borrowed HP (Team Mantle)
- **Experimental Design Award:** Parallax Drift (Team Slipstream)

Judges praised Signal//Lost for turning communication friction into compelling cooperative play.
